Separate from SudokuLog's so logging there does not block attesting here (and vice versa) \u2014 each CANONICAL record admits a solve exactly once."},"isPayable()":{"notice":"Checks if the resolver can be sent ETH."},"multiAttest((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint64,uint64,bytes32,address,address,bool,bytes)[],uint256[])":{"notice":"Processes multiple attestations and verifies whether they are valid."},"multiRevoke((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint64,uint64,bytes32,address,address,bool,bytes)[],uint256[])":{"notice":"Processes revocation of multiple attestation and verifies they can be revoked."},"revoke((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint64,uint64,bytes32,address,address,bool,bytes))":{"notice":"Processes an attestation revocation and verifies if it can be revoked."},"version()":{"notice":"Returns the full semver contract version."}},"notice":"EAS schema resolver for the ZK-Sudoku leaderboard \u2014 the SKILL_GAMES_DESIGN.md \"leaderboard = EAS attestation\" plan: a canonical leaderboard entry is an EAS attestation that can only exist if the solve proof checks out, because this resolver re-runs exactly the checks SudokuLog.logSolve performs (published-puzzle hash, fresh nullifier, PLONK verify) before letting the attestation through. EAS-native indexing then serves the leaderboard without a custom indexer; SudokuLog remains live as the pre-EAS record and neither path depends on the other (separate nullifier books on purpose \u2014 each canonical record spends a solve once). SCHEMA (field order is load-bearing \u2014 it IS the attestation encoding; register with revocable=false):   uint256 puzzleId,uint256 player,uint256 nullifier,uint256[24] proof,uint256[81] puzzle There is deliberately NO elapsed field: EAS stamps `attestation.time` natively, so readers derive elapsed = time - openedAt. An attester-supplied elapsed would either be trusted (wrong) or force the attester to predict the exact mined block timestamp (unusable). The proof is bound to `player` via the nullifier (see SudokuRules), so a mempool watcher cannot re-bind someone's solve; the attestation's `recipient`/`attester` are informational \u2014 the leaderboard identity is `player`, same as SudokuLog.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/eas/SudokuSolveResolver.sol":"SudokuSolveResolver"},"evmVersion":"shanghai","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":1000},"remappings":[":@ethereum-attestation-service/=node_modules/@ethereum-attestation-service/",":@openzeppelin/=node_modules/@openzeppelin/",":forge-std/=../../lib/forge-std/src/",":hardhat/=node_modules/hardhat/",":multicaller/=node_modules/multicaller/",":solady/=node_modules/solady/"],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"contracts/eas/SudokuSolveResolver.sol":{"keccak256":"0xc817efbaaa268d5df20c3fa76426dd0e3cd82d921efdc5f529933b0cb0a3bf78","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://5b8601ddcd0c6c542b14c69721b174f30563a90030194815ae9a71e941d3ea45","dweb:/ipfs/QmWETiWkcqAumeoV3grnMoQF1W9pB1SibJSmsiuReYBns7"]},"contracts/games/SudokuLog.sol":{"keccak256":"0xd8ccdbd356d8f53274ff86f319cede887224d8b4f178412b8b1673921a0542f9","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://48e9afa27b069a50194f425691de493c15b7182e2159185c8f69e2a85cca28b4","dweb:/ipfs/QmUvuQQaMi5BybfSdkLjS3Cu9CX52MXA4gTVtAfcr4e4co"]},"contracts/zk/SudokuRules.sol":{"keccak256":"0xb48dab2a552bcd5f902208870fd8c9ad9fd35d414ab47a89eda97627c99e1fd3","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://07329253e94d4f3ad88b702120051ccdbdfb4ff7a5610fb12f3b2ccbf7c9bb81","dweb:/ipfs/QmWYQEqJmBWmhae5xYthSL213WdiSQXqZAFWdwjaSZDj2g"]},"contracts/zk/generated/SudokuSolvePlonkVerifier.sol":{"keccak256":"0x8ac969e28b2168bc9d90adcd180ff8bff61e9faa33a2f59307b59b529b1cef95","license":"GPL-3.0","urls":["bzz-raw://09a6d3beca0a7be855423b958dd4d222d9c2e491f409e9312621134aba06f793","dweb:/ipfs/QmV4tQhjWxdYTZa77EkEeB3QvVEXijYLv5fAA2PZwrSUQy"]},"node_modules/@ethereum-attestation-service/eas-contracts/contracts/Common.sol":{"keccak256":"0x957bd2e6d0d6d637f86208b135c29fbaf4412cb08e5e7a61ede16b80561bf685","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://da1dc9aedbb1d4d39c46c2235918d3adfbc5741dd34a46010cf425d134e7936d","dweb:/ipfs/QmWUk6bXnLaghS2 returns._0: Whether the resolver supports ETH transfers.

params.values: Explicit ETH amounts which were sent with each attestation.

returns._0: Whether all the attestations are valid.

params.attestations: The existing attestations to be revoked.
params.values: Explicit ETH amounts which were sent with each revocation.

returns._0: Whether the attestations can be revoked.

params.attestation: The existing attestation to be revoked.

returns._0: Whether the attestation can be revoked.
Semver contract version as a string.

attest((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint64,ui.notice: Processes an attestation and verifies whether it's valid.

attestedNullifier(uint256).notice: This resolver's own spent book: one attestation per solve nullifier. Separate from SudokuLog's so logging there does not block attesting here (and vice versa) — each CANONICAL record admits a solve exactly once.

isPayable().notice: Checks if the resolver can be sent ETH.

multiAttest((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint.notice: Processes multiple attestations and verifies whether they are valid.

multiRevoke((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint.notice: Processes revocation of multiple attestation and verifies they can be revoked.

revoke((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint64,ui.notice: Processes an attestation revocation and verifies if it can be revoked.

version().notice: Returns the full semver contract version.
